title: Leon Thomas' Mutt Album Makes Historic Impact at 2026 Grammys
text: Leon Thomas' album Mutt was nominated for Album of the Year at the 2026 Grammys. All Album of the Year nominees, including Leon Thomas, were first-time nominees in this category, making this a historic year where the winner would be guaranteed to be a first-time Album of the Year winner. Mutt is Thomas' sophomore album, released in 2024 as a follow-up to his debut album Electric Dusk, drawing inspiration from artists ranging from Art Blakey and Miles Davis to Black Sabbath and Led Zeppelin. The 14-track project incorporates Jazz, Neo-Soul, and Rock influences. Leon Thomas scored two wins at the 68th Grammy Awards, winning Best R&B Album for his album Mutt, which was also nominated for Album of the Year. Bad Bunny ultimately won the Grammy for Album of the Year with DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS, a history-making album as the first non-English-language album to ever win the Grammy for Album Of The Year. Leon Thomas is the most-nominated R&B artist at the 2026 Grammy Awards with multiple nominations across major categories.
